title: rubus occidentalis subsp. euoccidentalis
intro: Rubus occidentalis subsp. euoccidentalis, also known as the western thimbleberry, is a subspecies of the black raspberry plant native to the western United States and Canada. It is a deciduous shrub with edible fruits that have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The western thimbleberry has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as diarrhea, fever, and skin conditions, and has been found to have potential health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. Western thimbleberry is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or jam, and can also be consumed fresh or frozen, although proper identification and preparation are essential to ensure its safe use as a medicinal herb.
